scope: WhisperFlow — Whisper Dictation macOS menu-bar app
methodology: 67 automated tests across 8 categories (anti-hallucination, cleaner, injector, performance, replacements, security, settings, stats/recorder/vad) + interactive smoke testing. Manual tests listed separately in final_report.md.

bugs_found_and_fixed_during_audit.title: Anti-hallucination filter missed 'Thank you very much' (common Whisper hallucination)
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_audit.steps_to_reproduce: filter_transcription('Thank you very much.') → returns 'Thank you very much.'
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_audit.expected: Returns '' — this phrase is the #1 Whisper hallucination on silent audio.
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_audit.actual: Returns the text unchanged. In production, this meant the user's silent microphone (mic-permission bug earlier) produced 'Thank you very much.' pasted into active apps.
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_audit.evidence: User's real dictation on 2026-04-18 logged 'Done (pasted): "Thank you very much."' while mic was silent (peak=0.000).

pre_existing_bugs_fixed_in_prior_QA_pass: 32 bugs found in earlier architectural review — all closed. Summary:
- 8 HIGH (races, clipboard overwrite, silent permission losses, etc.)
See git log commits ebc3c3e, 949cd0d, 5606a37 for details.

test_infrastructure_bugs_fixed.title: TC_043 search for 'os.system' matched comments in source, false positive
test_infrastructure_bugs_fixed.fix: Strip comments before scanning, require function-call syntax 'os.system('

not_applicable_categories.upload_audio: Product is mic-only; no upload path
not_applicable_categories.cross_user_access: Single-user local app; no auth, no server
not_applicable_categories.server_rate_limiting: No server; OpenAI API is upstream
not_applicable_categories.export_file: No file export — paste via Cmd+V is the export
